title: rubus inframollis
intro: Rubus inframollis, also known as the wild cane or thimbleberry, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family that is native to Eastern North America. The medicinal properties of Rubus inframollis include its high antioxidant and anti-inflammatory content, which has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Rubus inframollis include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, Rubus inframollis is often used to make teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing.
